title: Dubai 7-Day Luxury Escape
metaTitle: Dubai 7-Day Luxury Escape | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Dubai, United Arab Emirates: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Burj Khalifa (At the Top observation deck) and Dubai Mall Aquarium & Underwater Zoo. Prices from $2043 per person (estimate).
highlights: Burj Khalifa (At the Top observation deck)
Desert dune bashing and quad biking
Stay in an underwater or overwater suite at Atlantis The Palm
Abu Dhabi (Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque, Louvre Abu Dhabi)
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Dubai, United Arab Emirates — Desert climate with mild, sunny winters (highs 24°C) and extremely hot, humid summers (highs 41°C) where outdoor activity is largely limited to early morning or evening. November to March offers pleasantly warm, dry weather ideal for outdoor sightseeing, desert safaris and beach time. This itinerary blends Burj Khalifa (At the Top observation deck), Dubai Mall Aquarium & Underwater Zoo, Dubai Fountain show (standard viewing) with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Burj Khalifa (At the Top observation deck), Dubai Mall Aquarium & Underwater Zoo, Dubai Fountain show (standard viewing)
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Dubai
itinerary.description: Land at Dubai International Airport (DXB) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Burj Khalifa (At the Top observation deck) — World's tallest building with sky-high viewing decks. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.
activities: Burj Khalifa (At the Top observation deck)

itinerary.title: Dubai: Dubai Mall Aquarium & Underwater Zoo & Desert safari with dune bashing & BBQ dinner
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Dubai Mall Aquarium & Underwater Zoo — Massive aquarium tank inside the world's largest mall. In the afternoon, continue to Desert safari with dune bashing & BBQ dinner — Evening 4x4 desert tour with entertainment. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Desert safari with dune bashing & BBQ dinner

itinerary.title: Dubai: Palm Jumeirah / Atlantis Aquaventure Waterpark & Dubai Frame
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Palm Jumeirah / Atlantis Aquaventure Waterpark — Large waterpark on the iconic Palm island. In the afternoon, continue to Dubai Frame — Giant picture-frame structure with old and new Dubai views. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Abu Dhabi (Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque, Louvre Abu Dhabi)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Abu Dhabi (Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque, Louvre Abu Dhabi), a popular side trip from Dubai. Return to Dubai in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Abu Dhabi (Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque, Louvre Abu Dhabi)

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Al Seef heritage waterfront district — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Dubai: Museum of the Future & Dubai Fountain show viewing (VIP boardwalk)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Museum of the Future — Futuristic architecture and immersive innovation exhibits. In the afternoon, continue to Dubai Fountain show viewing (VIP boardwalk) — Premium viewing for the choreographed fountain show. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Ski Dubai or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to DXB for your departure flight.

travelTips: Visit outdoor attractions and do desert safaris between November and March to avoid extreme summer heat.
Book Burj Khalifa "At the Top" tickets for sunset slots — they sell out and offer the best views.
Malls double as social hubs with free entertainment (fountain shows, ice rinks) — budget time to explore them.
Alcohol is only available in licensed hotel bars/restaurants, not supermarkets, so plan accordingly.
Friday is the start of the weekend in the UAE — some government offices and services close.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Modest clothing for malls and public areas outside beach/pool zones
Light, breathable fabrics for extreme heat
A light jacket for heavily air-conditioned malls and restaurants
Sun protection for desert and beach excursions
thingsToDo: Burj Khalifa (At the Top observation deck)
Desert safari with dune bashing & BBQ dinner
Dhow dinner cruise on Dubai Creek/Marina
Desert dune bashing and quad biking
thingsToAvoid: Public displays of affection — even hand-holding beyond light contact can draw unwanted attention.
Photographing government buildings, military installations, or people without consent.
Drinking alcohol outside licensed venues, or being visibly intoxicated in public — both carry legal risk.
Overpacking the summer months with extensive outdoor plans — the heat can be genuinely dangerous.
Assuming all beaches are free — some upscale beach clubs charge a day-pass entry fee.
instagramSpots: Burj Al Arab from Jumeirah Beach
hiddenGems: Dubai Creek abra crossing at sunset
familyActivities: IMG Worlds of Adventure theme park
adventureActivities: Desert dune bashing and quad biking
luxuryExperiences: Stay in an underwater or overwater suite at Atlantis The Palm
Private yacht charter along the Marina and Palm
Fine dining at Pierchic overwater restaurant
Helicopter tour over the Palm and Burj Al Arab
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset dhow dinner cruise on Dubai Marina
Private desert camp dinner under the stars
Overwater suite stay at Atlantis The Palm
Champagne at At.mosphere atop the Burj Khalifa
budgetExperiences: Relax at public Jumeirah or Kite Beach
Explore Al Fahidi Historical Neighbourhood on foot
Abra boat crossing on Dubai Creek for a few dirhams
localEtiquette: Dress modestly in public areas and malls — shoulders and knees covered; swimwear is only appropriate at pools and beaches.
Public displays of affection are frowned upon and can be legally problematic — keep contact minimal.
During Ramadan, avoid eating, drinking or smoking in public during daylight hours.
Alcohol is only served in licensed venues (hotels, some restaurants) — public intoxication is illegal.
Photography of government buildings, military sites, or local people without permission should be avoided.
localTransportGuide: The Dubai Metro is modern, driverless and air-conditioned, connecting most major attractions along two lines with a Nol card for payment. Taxis and ride-hailing apps (Careem, Uber) are affordable and widely used for areas the metro doesn't reach, like Palm Jumeirah.
bestTimeToVisit: November to March offers pleasantly warm, dry weather ideal for outdoor sightseeing, desert safaris and beach time.
weather: Desert climate with mild, sunny winters (highs 24°C) and extremely hot, humid summers (highs 41°C) where outdoor activity is largely limited to early morning or evening.
visaInformation: Many nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens receive visa-on-arrival or a free 30/90-day visit visa depending on passport; some nationalities can also apply for an e-Visa in advance. Always verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.
averageFlightTime: ~9.3h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Dubai?
faqs.answer: Many nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens receive visa-on-arrival or a free 30/90-day visit visa depending on passport; some nationalities can also apply for an e-Visa in advance. Always verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Dubai?
faqs.answer: November to March offers pleasantly warm, dry weather ideal for outdoor sightseeing, desert safaris and beach time. Desert climate with mild, sunny winters (highs 24°C) and extremely hot, humid summers (highs 41°C) where outdoor activity is largely limited to early morning or evening.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Dubai safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Dubai is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Dubai?
faqs.answer: The local currency is AED. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: It also works well for families — highlights include Atlantis Aquaventure Waterpark.

reviews.author: Liam Costa
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Our trip to Dubai was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Jumeirah Beach was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Sofia Wang
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Kite Beach in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Isabella Botha
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Dubai Fountain show viewing (VIP boardwalk) and Gold Souk & Spice Souk. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Fatima Novak
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Dubai Mall Aquarium & Underwater Zoo alone was worth the trip, but combined with Ski Dubai this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Fatima Dubois
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: La Mer beachfront district alone was worth the trip, but combined with Ski Dubai this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Yuki Patel
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Dubai Fountain show (standard viewing) alone was worth the trip, but combined with Dubai Fountain show viewing (VIP boardwalk) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Arjun Costa
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. La Mer beachfront district in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Fatima Smith
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Burj Al Arab exterior viewpoint alone was worth the trip, but combined with Al Fahidi Historical Neighbourhood this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Nadia Sharma
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Dubai is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Jumeirah Beach. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Nadia Cohen
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
